title: Aurora Cannabis Acquires MedReleaf Australia
text: Aurora Cannabis acquired remaining approximately 90% equity interest of MedReleaf Australia at total enterprise value of AUD$50 million. Transaction announced on February 8, 2024. Aurora paid AUD$9.45 million in cash and issuance of approximately 69.5 million Aurora shares as consideration, with about 10% held in escrow. Financial terms: MedReleaf Australia generated trailing 12-month net revenue of approximately AUD$40 million as of December 31, 2023. Deal expected to be immediately accretive to Adjusted EBITDA and accelerate company's positive free cash flow objectives for 2024. Market position: MedReleaf Australia holds number two position in Australia's medical cannabis sector, valued at roughly AUD$400 million. Aurora's CEO stated this acquisition makes company the largest global medical cannabis company operating in nationally legal markets. Strategic rationale: Deal strengthens Aurora's presence in rapidly expanding market while leveraging pharmaceutical-grade cultivation capabilities. Australian model mirrors successful operations in Germany, Poland, and United Kingdom - markets characterized by clinician-led, medically-focused distribution. Management expects improved margins and revenue contributions aligned with established profitable operations. Since first partnering with MedReleaf Australia in 2017, Aurora actively contributed to region's recent growth, leveraging company's pharmaceutical grade cultivation and global approach to product innovation.
